jokes.text: Group chanting 'Reunion! Reunion!' repeatedly with increasing energy
jokes.setup: Characters getting pumped for high school reunion
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top chanting reveals their desperation for relevance
jokes.explanation: Establishes the pathetic desperation of the characters from the opening, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: That's a stupid song.
jokes.setup: Characters enthusiastically singing their high school fight song
jokes.punchline: Dennis abruptly dismisses it with bland contempt
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Dennis's narcissistic dismissal of shared experiences

jokes.text: it is gonna propel me back into the mid-'90s when Dennis Reynolds was a golden god
jokes.setup: Dennis talking about going to the reunion
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself in third person as a 'golden god'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis characterization - the narcissism and delusion are pitch-perfect

jokes.text: No brace, fools! Baby got back!
jokes.setup: Dee showing off her recovered back
jokes.punchline: Misappropriates Sir Mix-a-Lot reference inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Dee completely misunderstands the cultural reference

jokes.text: Sir Mix-a-Lot was not talking about women whose backs have recovered from horrific spinal disorder. He was talking about gorgeous black women and their big beautiful black butts.
jokes.punchline: Dennis's overly clinical and inappropriate correction
jokes.explanation: Dennis's awkward over-explanation creates good cringe comedy

jokes.text: Let's smash 'em up!
jokes.punchline: Grown adults still doing destructive high school ritual
jokes.explanation: Shows their arrested development through continued destructive behavior

jokes.text: I'm packing a couple extra pair of underwear in case I get an atomic wedgie.
jokes.punchline: Adult man genuinely afraid of high school bullying tactics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - childlike fear mixed with bizarre preparation

jokes.text: And if it does happen, I'm gonna get high, I'm gonna go to the bathroom, I'm gonna huff stuff.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions destructive coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Charlie's self-destructive tendencies delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Nah, I think he does. You guys were pretty much nothing in high school.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to build Charlie's confidence
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately undermines with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion of expected support - Mac's bluntness cuts through the moment

jokes.text: Frank being denied entry for not being faculty or alumni, then drinking alone in parking lot
jokes.setup: Frank trying to get into reunion
jokes.punchline: Obvious old man being rejected and relegated to parking lot drinking
jokes.explanation: Straightforward visual gag, functional but predictable

jokes.text: We smashed all the beers.
jokes.setup: Dennis suggesting Frank drink beer in parking lot
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals consequence of their pre-reunion ritual
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the logical consequence of their destructive ritual

jokes.text: Well, she was definitely the prettiest girl in school. She was not. She was not, no.
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate, emphatic denial
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic between Charlie's delusion and Mac's reality check

jokes.text: Maybe the most forgettable girl in school. Yeah, which, by the way, is the worst thing you could be in high school.
jokes.punchline: Dee's commentary on high school social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about high school hierarchy delivered with casual cruelty

jokes.text: Oh, the box of hornets.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about a 'present' Charlie gave
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of giving someone a box of hornets
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the casual delivery of something horrifically violent is perfect Charlie

jokes.text: It's not acne, actually. No, no, it's hornet scars.
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Charlie's 'present' caused permanent damage
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the horror of Charlie's past action

jokes.text: I'm probably not gonna wear mine. I mean, who wears a name tag, right? That's not cool.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking Mac to put on his name tag
jokes.punchline: Mac's insecurity about appearing uncool at a reunion
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's desperate attempt to appear cool in the lamest possible context

jokes.text: Mac's name tag reading 'Ronald McDonald' and his extreme discomfort
jokes.setup: Mac reluctantly putting on his high school name tag
jokes.punchline: Reveal that he was called 'Ronald McDonald' in school
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal showing Mac's high school humiliation, solid cringe humor

jokes.text: It's Adriano Calvaniste, Charlie. It's the guy who coined the name 'Aluminum monster.'
jokes.setup: Dee pointing out someone from high school
jokes.punchline: Reference to Dee's humiliating nickname
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dee's revenge plot while referencing her past humiliation

jokes.text: He called you Dirtgrub and he made you eat spiders. Oh, that guy!
jokes.punchline: Charlie's excited recognition of his abuser
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Charlie's twisted psychology - he's excited to see his bully

jokes.text: Yes. I mean, no. People don't call me that anymore, but yeah.
jokes.setup: Adriano asking if she's the aluminum monster
jokes.punchline: Dee's fumbled attempt to deny while confirming
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Dee's inability to smoothly handle social situations

jokes.text: Hey, Ronald McDonald, right? No, no. For your information, nobody calls me that.
jokes.punchline: Mac's defensive response to his embarrassing name
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Mac's continued embarrassment about his real name

jokes.text: 'Cause he was a nark and a tattletale, and he became the main drug dealer when he told on the other main drug dealer.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Mac was called 'Ronnie the Rat'
jokes.punchline: The ironic hypocrisy of Mac's drug dealing career
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reveal about Mac's opportunistic nature and moral flexibility

jokes.text: Well, looks like the rat got a little too much cheese, huh? Fat.
jokes.punchline: Mac's blunt, defensive response
jokes.explanation: Simple fat joke but Mac's defensive reaction adds character comedy

jokes.text: I put on mass. We eat a lot of cheese. To put on mass.
jokes.punchline: Mac's delusional explanation using bodybuilding terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - reframing obvious weight gain as intentional muscle building

jokes.text: I only gave ringworm to Charlie. I got it from my dog Poppins, and then Charlie and I were wrestling every day because we were trying out for the wrestling team
jokes.punchline: Mac's overly detailed, gross explanation
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out comedy with Mac's oblivious oversharing

jokes.text: Hope you suck each other's rotten peckers until you get mouth cancer.
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude and violent homophobic insult
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing the casual cruelty of high school bullies, though relies on shock value

jokes.text: For some reason, homophobia, it's just, it's awesome to these guys. I'm sewing myself in.
jokes.punchline: Dee's analytical observation about appealing to bullies
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Dee analyzes the social dynamics she's trying to exploit

jokes.text: So do do not talk to me for the rest of the night, and know that I won't be talking to you, because you lower my stock.
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating friendship like a business transaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - viewing relationships purely in terms of social value

jokes.text: Look, I got myself a name tag. I'm Nicki Potnick.
jokes.punchline: Frank stealing a female classmate's identity
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Frank's complete lack of awareness about his disguise

jokes.text: Okay, Frank, first off, Nicki Potnick is a girl, okay? And secondly, she's not 65 years old.
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out the obvious flaws in Frank's plan
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response highlighting the absurdity of Frank's plan

jokes.text: The dead tooth-- it spurted in my nose, it stinks. What is that supposed to be? That's where your monthly payments are going. That's a diamond tooth, Dennis.
jokes.punchline: Maureen's grotesque diamond-in-dead-tooth modification
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly gross visual gag that perfectly captures the show's commitment to grotesque humor

jokes.text: Put a diamond on anything that's dead, and it's still dead, Maureen. It's rotting.
jokes.setup: Maureen showing off her diamond tooth
jokes.punchline: Dennis's disgusted but logical response
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan observation that captures Dennis's revulsion while being oddly philosophical

jokes.text: I went to go find my name tag-- they didn't even have one. It's like-- it's like what, I never existed?
jokes.punchline: Cricket's existential crisis over missing name tag
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cricket's desperate need for validation and belonging

jokes.text: Well, actually it's Father Mara again. Is it? I cleaned up, yes, and I was welcomed back into the cloth.
jokes.punchline: Cricket's claim to have returned to the priesthood
jokes.explanation: Good irony given Cricket's obvious degraded state despite claims of redemption

jokes.text: So I tell you what, Crick, should I put my balls on your chin now or later, huh?
jokes.setup: Cricket trying to be dignified as a priest
jokes.punchline: Adriano's crude sexual threat to a supposed man of God
jokes.explanation: Crude shock humor that relies more on transgression than wit

jokes.text: Probably not. Sat behind you in Spanish, remember? Sat... sat right next to you in Algebra one.
jokes.setup: Waitress trying to get people to remember her
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempts to jog memories of mundane interactions
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about the desperation of the forgotten trying to be remembered

jokes.text: Wow, I just shook your dead tooth wife. I mean, I had to wash my hands and face.
jokes.punchline: Frank's disgusted reaction to the dead tooth
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the grossness of the dead tooth gag

jokes.text: Don't you play coy with me, you little bitch. I'm going to stay right here, and I'm going to wait for my minions to swarm me.
jokes.setup: Tim Murphy inviting Dennis to his table
jokes.punchline: Dennis's delusional response about having 'minions'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis delusion - treating high school classmates like loyal subjects

jokes.text: Bartender giving Charlie and Mac only 4 drink tickets total, leading to argument about the 'pretty boy' and 'longhair'
jokes.punchline: Mac and Charlie's pathetic attempt to intimidate the bartender
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac and Charlie's pathetic attempts at intimidation

jokes.text: Adriano made fake tickets. Those tickets are fake!
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie needing drink tickets
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately reverting to tattling behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Mac hasn't changed - still a tattletale when it suits him

jokes.text: Wow. Ronnie the Rat strikes again, huh?
jokes.setup: Mac tattling on the fake tickets
jokes.punchline: Adriano confirming Mac's unchanged nature
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the consistency of Mac's character flaws

jokes.text: I'm going to go to the bathroom, I'm going to find stuff to huff, I'm going to get high. If I'm the Dirtgrub, I'm going to be the Dirtgrub.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's decision to embrace his destructive reputation
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy about accepting one's worst impulses when society rejects you

jokes.text: Charlie, Charlie, don't put the bleach and the ammonia together! It's going to knock the shit out of you.
jokes.punchline: Mac warning about creating deadly chlorine gas
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation where Charlie's self-destructive behavior becomes genuinely dangerous

jokes.text: Yeah, probably. That's what these gay guys do, they just... knock each other out with poppers, and then find some kinda rabies-infested rodent to tickle each other's taints with.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Mac and Charlie are 'perving out' on unconscious Charlie
jokes.punchline: Adriano's bizarre, homophobic fantasy scenario
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that relies more on shock and homophobia than genuine wit

jokes.text: I've been waiting a long time for this.
jokes.setup: Brad confronting Charlie (unconscious) about the hornet incident
jokes.punchline: Brad's long-awaited revenge moment
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff showing consequences of Charlie's past cruelty

jokes.text: Mac and Frank getting wedgied and stuffed in lockers by Adriano and friends
jokes.setup: Adult reunion turning into high school bullying
jokes.punchline: Grown men getting wedgies and locker stuffing
jokes.explanation: Callback to Charlie's wedgie fears, shows nothing has changed since high school

jokes.text: It's her fault. She told me she loved me. We were supposed to be together forever.
jokes.setup: Cricket being accused of theft and having ringworm
jokes.punchline: Cricket's pathetic romantic delusion about Dee
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cricket's continued delusion about his relationship with Dee

jokes.text: Yeah, I told him I loved him once, but that was like four years ago.
jokes.punchline: Dee's casual dismissal of what was obviously meaningful to Cricket
jokes.explanation: Good cringe showing Dee's casual cruelty and Cricket's romantic desperation

jokes.text: Cricket's revelation about stealing jewelry and being covered in ringworm after Dee told him she loved him
jokes.punchline: Cricket stole everything because Dee gave him false hope
jokes.explanation: Good reveal tying together Cricket's breakdown and Dee's manipulation

jokes.text: To think I was going to let you jack me off...
jokes.punchline: Adriano's crude revelation about his plans with Dee
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that relies mainly on shock value rather than clever construction
